test_name: Loans with missing PD, LGD or EAD inputs
audit_interpretation: Missing IFRS 9 model inputs may affect the completeness and reliability of expected credit loss calculations.

test_name: Reported RWA differs from simplified recalculated RWA
audit_interpretation: Differences between reported and recalculated RWA may indicate calculation, mapping or reconciliation issues requiring further audit investigation.

test_name: Reported ECL differs from simplified recalculated ECL
audit_interpretation: Differences between reported and recalculated ECL may indicate model input, calculation or reconciliation issues requiring further review.
RAF breaches indicate areas where current risk levels exceed approved appetite and require governance attention.
High funding concentration may increase vulnerability to counterparty withdrawal or market stress.

test_name: Material risks without assigned internal capital
audit_interpretation: Material risks without assigned capital may indicate weaknesses in ICAAP completeness, risk quantification or governance.

test_name: Material risks without assigned risk owner
audit_interpretation: Material risks without clear ownership may weaken accountability, monitoring and escalation within the risk governance framework.

test_name: Negative liquidity gaps by maturity bucket
audit_interpretation: Negative liquidity gaps in maturity buckets may indicate liquidity mismatch requiring monitoring, escalation or contingency funding actions.

test_name: RAF breaches not escalated to committee
audit_interpretation: Risk appetite breaches without escalation may indicate weaknesses in governance discipline and breach management procedures.

test_name: Non-eligible assets identified in liquidity buffer population
audit_interpretation: Non-eligible assets within the liquidity buffer population may indicate classification or data quality issues.
Missing regulatory exposure classifications may indicate data quality weaknesses affecting RWA calculation and prudential reporting.

test_name: Loans with 30+ days past due still classified as Stage 1
audit_interpretation: Loans with 30+ days past due remaining in Stage 1 may indicate staging inconsistencies or delayed recognition of credit deterioration.
Missing risk weights may prevent accurate RWA calculation and weaken regulatory reporting controls.

test_name: Loans with 90+ days past due not classified as Stage 3
audit_interpretation: Loans with default indicators not classified as Stage 3 may indicate weaknesses in default identification or staging controls.
